nativeRange.habitat: Seaward reefs below the surge zone, commonly near caves, overhangs, and ledges; recorded across a broad depth range from shallow reef flats down to roughly 200 m.

diet.notes: In the wild has been reported to feed on small crustaceans (shrimps, crabs), worms, and small fishes, hunting primarily at night. In captivity generally accepts a varied meaty diet of mysis, chopped marine meats, and frozen shrimp; newly-introduced individuals sometimes need live saltwater feeder shrimp to initiate feeding. Feeding two to three times daily is commonly advised.

avoidWith: small fish that fit in its mouth

summary: A nocturnal squirrelfish endemic to Hawaii and Johnston Atoll, reaching about 17 cm; bright red with thin white horizontal stripes. Generally hardy but tends to prey on small fish and motile invertebrates, so it is not strictly reef safe.
careNotes: The Striped Squirrelfish is a nocturnal species that generally hides in caves, overhangs, and ledges during the day and becomes active at dusk. It tends to do best with ample rockwork plus open swimming space, and a tightly-sealed lid is generally advised since squirrelfishes have been known to jump. Caution is warranted when netting or handling: the species carries sharp opercular and preopercular spines reported as venomous in the Holocentridae family, and its rough scales easily snag in netting. Considered reef safe with caution — it has been reported to consume small fish, ornamental shrimp, snails, serpent stars, and small worms, though sessile corals are generally left alone. Conspecifics can be kept together when introduced simultaneously to a sufficiently large aquarium with multiple hides. Sources disagree noticeably on minimum tank size: LiveAquaria lists 50–70 US gal (~190–265 L), AquaticCommunity 70 gal (~265 L), and Reefapp 600 L; 400 L was chosen as a cautious round value reflecting the species' active swimming, nocturnal predatory habit, and adult size. Care-level ratings span easy (LiveAquaria, AquaticCommunity) through moderate (LiveAquaria species page) to implied moderate (Reefapp); intermediate is the averaged assessment. Adult size estimates range from ~15 cm (LiveAquaria's 6 in captive max) to 17 cm (FishBase/Wikipedia max TL). Temperament is described as peaceful by some hobby retailers but as 'might be aggressive' by Reefapp; semi-aggressive better reflects the species' predator-on-small-tankmates behavior. Specific sex-change behavior was not confirmed in reviewed sources for this species and so protogyny is left unflagged. RESOLVED 2026-06-03: Two open concerns were verified against additional sources. (1) Venomous spines: Confirmed. The preopercular/opercular spines of Sargocentron (subfamily Holocentrinae) are documented as venomous across multiple independent references — the family-level literature confirms venom glands in opercular spines, LiveAquaria lists 'Venomous: Yes', and saltwateraquariumblog.com explicitly notes 'The Holocentrinae carry venom in the spines on their opercular spines.' The fish.venomousSpines=true field is correct. (2) Minimum tank size 400 L: Retained. A fifth source (saltwateraquariumblog.com) recommends 75–100 US gal (~284–379 L) as a general squirrelfish baseline; this narrows the credible range to 284–600 L with an outlier-adjusted centre near 350–400 L. The 400 L figure (a valid nice-values entry) is defensible and is kept unchanged. LiveAquaria's 50 gal figure appears to be a trade-floor minimum, not an optimal recommendation for a 17 cm active nocturnal predator.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species-specific product page; provided care level, temperament, reef-compat, water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), tank size hints, and diet.

additional.name: fishbase.mnhn.fr
additional.notes: Designated secondary returned HTTP 403; substituted with FishBase data via Wikipedia plus three additional hobby/encyclopedia sources to satisfy the cross-source verification rule.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Holocentridae), distribution (Hawaii + Johnston Atoll), depth range 1–217 m, max TL 17 cm, nocturnal habit, diet of worms/crustaceans/starfish, IUCN Least Concern.

additional.name: Reefapp.net
additional.notes: Cross-checked max size (17 cm), reef-safe with caution, conspecific tolerance with simultaneous introduction, and a much larger 600 L minimum tank recommendation versus the LiveAquaria 70 gal figure.

additional.name: AquaticCommunity
additional.notes: Cross-checked native range, water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025), tank size 70 gal (~265 L), nocturnal behavior, predator-on-inverts diet, and care level.
